ESP32S3_APC_UPS_progress_summary_v8 (1).txt
Document
Continuation of the esp32-s3 to act as a Nut Network ups tool attached is the progress in text file. and a copy of v8 of the main.c config pasted /* main.c CURRENT VERSION: v8 * Version tracking: * v8 (2026-03-05): Milestone 5 start: maintain decoded UPS state + NUT-style status logs. * Disable full HID report descriptor dump by default (V7 control submit ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG). * Keep usb_host_lib_handle_events() pump + async client callback + change-only IN logging. */ #include <stdio.h> #include <string.h> #include <inttypes.h> #include <stdbool.h> #include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h" #include "freertos/task.h" #include "freertos/semphr.h" #include "esp_log.h" #include "esp_err.h" #include "usb/usb_host.h" #include "usb/usb_types_ch9.h" // ===================== REVERT INDEX ===================== // REVERT[M3-A-001]: (2026-03-05) Use async client callback + usb_host_client_handle_events(client, timeout) (2 args). // REVERT[M3-A-002]: (2026-03-05) Do NOT use usb_device_info_t.idVendor/idProduct (fields differ). Use usb_host_get_device_descriptor() (ptr-to-ptr). // REVERT[M3-A-003]: (2026-03-05) IDF v5.3.1 transfer type is usb_transfer_t (NOT usb_host_transfer_t). // REVERT[M3-A-004]: (2026-03-05) Endpoint direction test uses (ep & 0x80) instead of missing macros. // REVERT[M3-A-005]: (2026-03-05) XFERTYPE mask macro is USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFERTYPE_MASK (not *_M). // REVERT[M3-A-006]: (2026-03-05) NEW_DEV address saved from callback, not hardcoded. // REVERT[M3-A-007]: (2026-03-05) No usb_host_transfer_cancel / usb_host_release_config_descriptor in v5.3.1. // REVERT[M3-IN-001]: (2026-03-05) Change-only logging for interrupt IN reports. // REVERT[M4-D-001]: (2026-03-05) Decode report IDs 0x0C batt/runtime, 0x13 AC, 0x16 flags (little-endian). // ========================================================= static const char *TAG = "UPS_USB_M3"; #define UPS_VID 0x051d #define UPS_PID 0x0002 // HID descriptor types #define USB_DESC_TYPE_HID 0x21 #define USB_DESC_TYPE_HID_REPORT 0x22 // v8: disable by default because V7 hit ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG on submit (likely size/path constraint) #define ENABLE_REPORT_DESCRIPTOR_DUMP 0 static inline uint16_t rd_le16(const uint8_t *p) { return (uint16_t)(p[0] | ((uint16_t)p[1] << 8)); } static inline uint32_t rd_le32(const uint8_t *p) { return (uint32_t)(p[0] | ((uint32_t)p[1] << 8) | ((uint32_t)p[2] << 16) | ((uint32_t)p[3] << 24)); } // USB host handles static usb_host_client_handle_t s_client = NULL; static usb_device_handle_t s_dev = NULL; // Device address from NEW_DEV static volatile uint8_t s_new_dev_addr = 0; // HID interface/endpoint static int s_hid_intf_num = -1; static uint8_t s_ep_in_addr = 0; static uint16_t s_ep_in_mps = 0; static uint16_t s_hid_report_desc_len = 0; // Interrupt IN tracking static uint8_t s_last_in[64]; static int s_last_in_len = -1; // Event flags static volatile bool s_dev_connected = false; static volatile bool s_dev_gone = false; /* ========================================================= * JOB TASK: Milestone 5 state model (minimal) * ========================================================= */ typedef struct { bool have_batt; bool have_runtime; bool have_ac; bool have_flags; uint8_t battery_charge_pct; // from 0x0C byte1 uint32_t battery_runtime_s; // from 0x0C bytes2..3 (u16) but store in u32 uint8_t ac_present; // from 0x13 byte1 (0/1) uint32_t flags; // from 0x16 bytes1..4 (u32) // Derived (best-effort) bool on_battery; // derived from AC_present (if known) } ups_state_t; static ups_state_t s_state = {0}; static ups_state_t s_state_last_printed = {0}; static bool state_equals_for_print(const ups_state_t *a, const ups_state_t *b) { return (a->have_batt == b->have_batt) && (a->have_runtime == b->have_runtime) && (a->have_ac == b->have_ac) && (a->have_flags == b->have_flags) && (a->battery_charge_pct == b->battery_charge_pct) && (a->battery_runtime_s == b->battery_runtime_s) && (a->ac_present == b->ac_present) && (a->flags == b->flags) && (a->on_battery == b->on_battery); } static void update_derived_state(ups_state_t *st) { if (st->have_ac) { st->on_battery = (st->ac_present == 0); } } static void log_nut_style_if_changed(void) { if (!state_equals_for_print(&s_state, &s_state_last_printed)) { s_state_last_printed = s_state; // Best-effort NUT-ish status: OL vs OB const char *ups_status = "UNKNOWN"; if (s_state.have_ac) { ups_status = s_state.on_battery ? "OB" : "OL"; } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "----- NUT-ish status (Milestone 5) -----"); if (s_state.have_batt) { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.charge: %u", (unsigned)s_state.battery_charge_pct); } else { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.charge: (unknown)"); } if (s_state.have_runtime) { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.runtime: %" PRIu32, s_state.battery_runtime_s); } else { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.runtime: (unknown)"); } if (s_state.have_ac) { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "input.utility.present: %u", (unsigned)s_state.ac_present); } else { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "input.utility.present: (unknown)"); } if (s_state.have_flags) { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.flags: 0x%08" PRIX32, s_state.flags); } else { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.flags: (unknown)"); } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.status: %s", ups_status); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "---------------------------------------"); } } // Control transfer sync typedef struct { SemaphoreHandle_t done; esp_err_t status; int actual_num_bytes; } ctrl_wait_t; // REVERT[M3-A-003]: transfer type is usb_transfer_t in IDF v5.3.1. static void ctrl_xfer_cb(usb_transfer_t *t) { ctrl_wait_t *w = (ctrl_wait_t *)t->context; if (w) { w->status = t->status; w->actual_num_bytes = (int)t->actual_num_bytes; xSemaphoreGive(w->done); } } static void intr_in_cb(usb_transfer_t *t); // ---------- USB Host client event callback ---------- static void client_event_cb(const usb_host_client_event_msg_t *event_msg, void *arg) { (void)arg; if (!event_msg) return; switch (event_msg->event) { case USB_HOST_CLIENT_EVENT_NEW_DEV: s_new_dev_addr = event_msg->new_dev.address; // REVERT[M3-A-006] s_dev_connected = true; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "NEW_DEV received: addr=%u", (unsigned)s_new_dev_addr); break; case USB_HOST_CLIENT_EVENT_DEV_GONE: s_dev_gone = true; ESP_LOGW(TAG, "DEV_GONE received"); break; default: break; } } // ---------- Descriptor parsing ---------- static bool parse_hid_intf_and_ep(const uint8_t *cfg, int cfg_len, int *out_intf, uint8_t *out_ep_in, uint16_t *out_mps, uint16_t *out_hid_rpt_len) { int cur_intf = -1; bool in_hid_intf = false; uint16_t hid_rpt_len = 0; uint8_t ep_in = 0; uint16_t ep_mps = 0; int i = 0; while (i + 2 <= cfg_len) { uint8_t bLength = cfg[i]; uint8_t bDescriptorType = cfg[i + 1]; if (bLength < 2) break; if (i + bLength > cfg_len) break; if (bDescriptorType == USB_B_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_INTERFACE && bLength >= sizeof(usb_intf_desc_t)) { const usb_intf_desc_t *intf = (const usb_intf_desc_t *)&cfg[i]; cur_intf = intf->bInterfaceNumber; in_hid_intf = (intf->bInterfaceClass == USB_CLASS_HID); if (in_hid_intf) { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID interface intf=%u alt=%u subclass=0x%02x proto=0x%02x", (unsigned)intf->bInterfaceNumber, (unsigned)intf->bAlternateSetting, (unsigned)intf->bInterfaceSubClass, (unsigned)intf->bInterfaceProtocol); } } else if (bDescriptorType == USB_DESC_TYPE_HID && in_hid_intf && bLength >= 9) { // HID descriptor: [6]=descType [7..8]=descLen (first class descriptor) uint8_t descType = cfg[i + 6]; uint16_t descLen = rd_le16(&cfg[i + 7]); if (descType == USB_DESC_TYPE_HID_REPORT) { hid_rpt_len = descLen; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID Report Descriptor length = %u bytes (from HID descriptor 0x21)", (unsigned)hid_rpt_len); } } else if (bDescriptorType == USB_B_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_ENDPOINT && in_hid_intf && bLength >= sizeof(usb_ep_desc_t)) { const usb_ep_desc_t *ep = (const usb_ep_desc_t *)&cfg[i]; // REVERT[M3-A-005]: use USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFERTYPE_MASK const uint8_t xfer_type = (ep->bmAttributes & USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFERTYPE_MASK); // REVERT[M3-A-004]: IN direction test uses bit7 (0x80) const bool is_in = ((ep->bEndpointAddress & 0x80) != 0); if ((xfer_type == USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFER_INT) && is_in) { ep_in = ep->bEndpointAddress; ep_mps = ep->wMaxPacketSize; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Interrupt IN EP=0x%02x MPS=%u", (unsigned)ep_in, (unsigned)ep_mps); } } i += bLength; } if (cur_intf >= 0 && ep_in != 0 && ep_mps != 0 && hid_rpt_len != 0) { *out_intf = cur_intf; *out_ep_in = ep_in; *out_mps = ep_mps; *out_hid_rpt_len = hid_rpt_len; return true; } return false; } // ---------- Control GET_DESCRIPTOR (Report) ---------- static esp_err_t usb_control_get_descriptor(usb_device_handle_t dev, uint8_t bmRequestType, uint8_t bRequest, uint16_t wValue, uint16_t wIndex, uint8_t *out, int out_len, int *out_actual) { if (!dev || !out || out_len <= 0) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG; usb_transfer_t *t = NULL; // REVERT[M3-A-003] const int xfer_len = (int)sizeof(usb_setup_packet_t) + out_len; esp_err_t err = usb_host_transfer_alloc(xfer_len, 0, &t); if (err != ESP_OK || !t) return err; ctrl_wait_t w = { .done = xSemaphoreCreateBinary(), .status = ESP_FAIL, .actual_num_bytes = 0, }; if (!w.done) { usb_host_transfer_free(t); return ESP_ERR_NO_MEM; } usb_setup_packet_t *setup = (usb_setup_packet_t *)t->data_buffer; setup->bmRequestType = bmRequestType; setup->bRequest = bRequest; setup->wValue = wValue; setup->wIndex = wIndex; setup->wLength = (uint16_t)out_len; t->device_handle = dev; t->bEndpointAddress = 0; // EP0 t->callback = ctrl_xfer_cb; t->context = &w; t->num_bytes = xfer_len; err = usb_host_transfer_submit(t); if (err != ESP_OK) { vSemaphoreDelete(w.done); usb_host_transfer_free(t); return err; } xSemaphoreTake(w.done, portMAX_DELAY); err = w.status; if (err == ESP_OK) { int got = w.actual_num_bytes - (int)sizeof(usb_setup_packet_t); if (got < 0) got = 0; if (got > out_len) got = out_len; memcpy(out, (uint8_t *)t->data_buffer + sizeof(usb_setup_packet_t), got); if (out_actual) *out_actual = got; } vSemaphoreDelete(w.done); usb_host_transfer_free(t); return err; } static void hid_get_report_descriptor_and_dump_once(void) { #if ENABLE_REPORT_DESCRIPTOR_DUMP if (!s_dev || s_hid_intf_num < 0 || s_hid_report_desc_len == 0) return; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Descriptor dump enabled (v8): attempting GET_DESCRIPTOR(report)."); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Requesting HID Report Descriptor via control transfer: intf=%d len=%u", s_hid_intf_num, (unsigned)s_hid_report_desc_len); uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)heap_caps_malloc(s_hid_report_desc_len, MALLOC_CAP_DEFAULT); if (!buf) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "malloc failed for report descriptor (%u)", (unsigned)s_hid_report_desc_len); return; } int actual = 0; esp_err_t err = usb_control_get_descriptor( s_dev, 0x81, 0x06, (uint16_t)((USB_DESC_TYPE_HID_REPORT << 8) | 0x00), (uint16_t)s_hid_intf_num, buf, (int)s_hid_report_desc_len, &actual ); if (err != ESP_OK) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "GET_DESCRIPTOR(report) failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err)); heap_caps_free(buf); return; } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID Report Descriptor received: %d bytes", actual); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "\n===== HID REPORT DESCRIPTOR BEGIN ====="); for (int i = 0; i < actual; i += 16) { char line[16 * 3 + 1]; int pos = 0; int n = (actual - i) > 16 ? 16 : (actual - i); for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) { pos += snprintf(&line[pos], sizeof(line) - pos, "%02X%s", buf[i + j], (j == n - 1) ? "" : " "); } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "%s", line); } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "===== HID REPORT DESCRIPTOR END =====\n"); heap_caps_free(buf); #else // v8 default: skip to avoid V7 error spam; we already have real IN reports for Milestone 5. (void)0; #endif } // ---------- Interrupt IN Reader ---------- static void decode_and_update_state(const uint8_t *d, int len) { if (!d || len <= 0) return; uint8_t rid = d[0]; if (rid == 0x0C && len >= 4) { uint8_t batt = d[1]; uint16_t runtime_s = rd_le16(&d[2]); s_state.battery_charge_pct = batt; s_state.battery_runtime_s = (uint32_t)runtime_s; s_state.have_batt = true; s_state.have_runtime = true; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "DECODE 0x0C: batt=%u%% runtime=%u s", (unsigned)batt, (unsigned)runtime_s); } else if (rid == 0x13 && len >= 2) { uint8_t ac = d[1]; s_state.ac_present = ac; s_state.have_ac = true; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "DECODE 0x13: AC_present=%u", (unsigned)ac); } else if (rid == 0x16 && len >= 5) { uint32_t flags = rd_le32(&d[1]); s_state.flags = flags; s_state.have_flags = true; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "DECODE 0x16: flags=0x%08" PRIX32, flags); } update_derived_state(&s_state); log_nut_style_if_changed(); } static void intr_in_cb(usb_transfer_t *t) { if (!t) return; if (t->status == ESP_OK && t->actual_num_bytes > 0) { const uint8_t *d = (const uint8_t *)t->data_buffer; int len = (int)t->actual_num_bytes; bool changed = (len != s_last_in_len) || (memcmp(d, s_last_in, (size_t)((len > (int)sizeof(s_last_in)) ? sizeof(s_last_in) : len)) != 0); if (changed) { // REVERT[M3-IN-001]: change-only logging s_last_in_len = len; int copy_len = len; if (copy_len > (int)sizeof(s_last_in)) copy_len = (int)sizeof(s_last_in); memcpy(s_last_in, d, (size_t)copy_len); char line[64 * 3 + 1]; int pos = 0; for (int i = 0; i < len && i < 64 && pos < (int)sizeof(line) - 4; i++) { pos += snprintf(&line[pos], sizeof(line) - pos, "%02X%s", d[i], (i == len - 1) ? "" : " "); } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID IN changed (%d): %s", len, line); decode_and_update_state(d, len); } } if (!s_dev_gone) { (void)usb_host_transfer_submit(t); } else { usb_host_transfer_free(t); } } static esp_err_t start_interrupt_in_reader(void) { if (!s_dev || s_ep_in_addr == 0 || s_ep_in_mps == 0) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE; usb_transfer_t *t = NULL; esp_err_t err = usb_host_transfer_alloc(s_ep_in_mps, 0, &t); if (err != ESP_OK || !t) return err; t->device_handle = s_dev; t->bEndpointAddress = s_ep_in_addr; t->callback = intr_in_cb; t->context = NULL; t->num_bytes = s_ep_in_mps; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting interrupt IN reader: EP=0x%02x MPS=%u (change-only)", (unsigned)s_ep_in_addr, (unsigned)s_ep_in_mps); return usb_host_transfer_submit(t); } /* ========================================================= * JOB TASK: USB host library event pump * Why: Needed for enumeration/host actions so NEW_DEV events fire. * ========================================================= */ static void usb_lib_task(void *arg) { (void)arg; while (1) { uint32_t flags = 0; esp_err_t err = usb_host_lib_handle_events(portMAX_DELAY, &flags); if (err != ESP_OK) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "usb_host_lib_handle_events: %s", esp_err_to_name(err)); } } } /* ========================================================= * JOB TASK: Main USB client task (open device, claim interface, start reader) * ========================================================= */ static void app_main_usb(void *arg) { (void)arg; usb_host_config_t host_cfg = { .skip_phy_setup = false, .intr_flags = ESP_INTR_FLAG_LEVEL1, }; ESP_ERROR_CHECK(usb_host_install(&host_cfg)); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "usb_host_install OK"); // Start the library pump (critical for enumeration) xTaskCreatePinnedToCore(usb_lib_task, "usb_lib", 4096, NULL, 20, NULL, 0); usb_host_client_config_t client_cfg = { .is_synchronous = false, .max_num_event_msg = 8, .async = { .client_event_callback = client_event_cb, .callback_arg = NULL, }, }; ESP_ERROR_CHECK(usb_host_client_register(&client_cfg, &s_client)); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "usb_host_client_register OK"); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Ready. Plug UPS into OTG port now (VBUS must be powered)."); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No polling spam: logs will appear only on USB events / changed HID reports."); while (1) { (void)usb_host_client_handle_events(s_client, portMAX_DELAY); if (s_dev_connected) { s_dev_connected = false; if (s_new_dev_addr == 0) { ESP_LOGW(TAG, "NEW_DEV flag set but addr=0? skipping"); continue; } usb_device_handle_t dev = NULL; esp_err_t err = usb_host_device_open(s_client, s_new_dev_addr, &dev); if (err != ESP_OK) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "usb_host_device_open(addr=%u) failed: %s", (unsigned)s_new_dev_addr, esp_err_to_name(err)); continue; } s_dev = dev; // REVERT[M3-A-002]: get_device_descriptor returns const ptr via ptr-to-ptr const usb_device_desc_t *dev_desc = NULL; err = usb_host_get_device_descriptor(s_dev, &dev_desc); if (err != ESP_OK || !dev_desc) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "get_device_descriptor failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err)); usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev); s_dev = NULL; continue; } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected VID:PID=%04x:%04x bDeviceClass=0x%02x EP0_MPS=%u", (unsigned)dev_desc->idVendor, (unsigned)dev_desc->idProduct, (unsigned)dev_desc->bDeviceClass, (unsigned)dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize0); if (dev_desc->idVendor != UPS_VID || dev_desc->idProduct != UPS_PID) { ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Not target UPS, closing."); usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev); s_dev = NULL; continue; } const usb_config_desc_t *cfg = NULL; err = usb_host_get_active_config_descriptor(s_dev, &cfg); if (err != ESP_OK || !cfg) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "get_active_config_descriptor failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err)); usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev); s_dev = NULL; continue; } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Active config wTotalLength=%u", (unsigned)cfg->wTotalLength); s_hid_intf_num = -1; s_ep_in_addr = 0; s_ep_in_mps = 0; s_hid_report_desc_len = 0; bool ok = parse_hid_intf_and_ep((const uint8_t *)cfg, cfg->wTotalLength, &s_hid_intf_num, &s_ep_in_addr, &s_ep_in_mps, &s_hid_report_desc_len); if (!ok) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to locate HID interface/EP/report-len in config descriptor"); usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev); s_dev = NULL; continue; } err = usb_host_interface_claim(s_client, s_dev, s_hid_intf_num, 0); if (err != ESP_OK) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Interface claim failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err)); usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev); s_dev = NULL; continue; } ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Interface claimed"); // v8 default: no descriptor dump; can enable via ENABLE_REPORT_DESCRIPTOR_DUMP hid_get_report_descriptor_and_dump_once(); // Reset milestone-5 state on attach memset(&s_state, 0, sizeof(s_state)); memset(&s_state_last_printed, 0, sizeof(s_state_last_printed)); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 5: state reset on attach."); err = start_interrupt_in_reader(); if (err != ESP_OK) { ESP_LOGE(TAG, "start_interrupt_in_reader failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err)); } } if (s_dev_gone) { s_dev_gone = false; if (s_dev) { if (s_hid_intf_num >= 0) { (void)usb_host_interface_release(s_client, s_dev, s_hid_intf_num); } (void)usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev); s_dev = NULL; } s_new_dev_addr = 0; s_hid_intf_num = -1; s_ep_in_addr = 0; s_ep_in_mps = 0; s_hid_report_desc_len = 0; s_last_in_len = -1; memset(s_last_in, 0, sizeof(s_last_in)); memset(&s_state, 0, sizeof(s_state)); memset(&s_state_last_printed, 0, sizeof(s_state_last_printed)); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 5: state cleared on detach."); } } } void app_main(void) { ESP_LOGI(TAG, "======================================="); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 3: HID report monitor (change-only)"); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 4: decode (0x0C batt/runtime, 0x13 AC, 0x16 flags)"); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 5: state + NUT-ish status logs"); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ESP-IDF %s", IDF_VER); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "main.c CURRENT VERSION: v8"); ESP_LOGI(TAG, "======================================="); xTaskCreatePinnedToCore(app_main_usb, "app_main_usb", 8192, NULL, 10, NULL, 0); }

ChatGPT said:

Got it — I read your attached progress summary and it lines up with the pasted main.c v8: you’re reliably enumerating the APC UPS (051d:0002), parsing HID intf/EP from the active config, streaming interrupt IN EP 0x81 (MPS 8) with change-only logging, decoding 0x0C / 0x13 / 0x16, and maintaining a minimal “NUT-ish” state print that updates only when values change.

Below is an updated “carry-forward” summary you can paste into the top of the summary text for starting new conversations (includes v8 success + forward plan, with more concrete next actions).

ESP32-S3 + APC UPS (USB HID) → NUT node — Progress Summary (carry-forward, v8 SUCCESS)

Date: 2026-03-05 (America/New_York)
Goal: ESP32-S3 acts as a small LAN “NUT node”: USB OTG host to APC UPS (HID), decode status reliably, then expose status over LAN.

What’s confirmed working (v8 = stable baseline)

USB host lifecycle is reliable

  • usb_host_install + async client registration works.

  • NEW_DEV event fires, device opens by address, DEV_GONE handled cleanly.

  • Replug cycles repeat without getting stuck; state resets on attach and clears on detach.

Descriptor parsing is reliable (from active config descriptor)

  • Active config wTotalLength=34

  • HID interface found: intf 0 alt 0

  • HID report descriptor length parsed from HID descriptor 0x21: 1049 bytes

  • Interrupt IN EP found: 0x81 MPS 8

  • Interface claim succeeds.

Interrupt IN streaming is stable

  • Continuous reports with minimal spam (logs only on change).

  • Common report IDs seen: 0x06, 0x0C, 0x13, 0x14, 0x16, 0x21.

Decoding implemented and verified (Milestone 4)

  • 0x0C: battery charge % + runtime seconds (little-endian u16 stored into u32)

  • 0x13: AC present (0/1)

  • 0x16: flags (little-endian u32)

  • Example values observed: batt ~98%, runtime ~35280s, AC_present=1, flags=0x0000000D.

State model + “NUT-ish” status print (Milestone 5)

  • Maintains last decoded values; prints a status block only when state changes.

  • ups.status is conservative:

    • AC_present=1 → “OL”

    • AC_present=0 → “OB”

    • unknown until AC is known.

Known issue (de-prioritized for now)

  • v7 attempted control GET_DESCRIPTOR(report) and got ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG; v8 disables full report descriptor dump by default.

  • Not blocking because report descriptor length is already parsed from HID descriptor (0x21) and IN reports are flowing.

Forward plan (next milestones)

v9 — Milestone 6: polish + extend decoding (USB-only, keep stable baseline)

Primary goals

  1. Keep change-only logging and clean attach/detach behavior exactly as-is (don’t regress stability).

  2. Add structured logging/decoding for “unknown but frequent” report IDs:

    • 0x06: likely heartbeat/status tuple → parse candidate fields (even if not fully understood)

    • 0x14 / 0x21: keep as raw but consistently formatted (RID + bytes).

  3. Improve ups.status composition (still conservative):

    • AC_present==1 → include “OL”

    • AC_present==0 → include “OB”

    • Optionally append extra tokens later (LB, CHRG, DISCHRG…) once flags are mapped.

  4. Flags work: keep raw hex, but start a bit breakdown print to map meanings experimentally.

Convenience / noise control

  • Reduce ESP-IDF USB internal debug chatter if desired:

    • esp_log_level_set("USBH", ESP_LOG_WARN);

    • esp_log_level_set("HUB", ESP_LOG_WARN);

Concrete v9 implementation ideas (safe, low-risk)

  • Add a small per-RID counter + last-seen timestamp (uptime ms) so you can see “are we still alive?” without spamming.

  • Add “state freshness” tracking: mark each decoded field with last_update_ms; if it goes stale, optionally print (... stale) in the NUT-ish block.

v10 — Milestone 7: LAN exposure (simple, readable, minimal UI)

Primary goals

  • Add a lightweight HTTP server for LAN:

    • GET /status → JSON (mirrors your internal ups_state_t)

    • Optional: GET /healthz → “ok”

    • Optional: GET /raw → last seen raw IN report (hex) + last RID seen

  • Add a minimal configuration mechanism:

    • Device label / node name

    • Auth (basic auth is fine to start)

    • Network config (static IP in 10.0.0.x/16 if that’s your LAN convention)

  • Keep your workflow constraint: assistant suggests edits; you apply them manually.

NUT-compat direction (later, once LAN basics are stable)

  • Map decoded fields to a NUT-ish vocabulary more fully:

    • battery.charge, battery.runtime, input.utility.present, ups.status, etc.

  • Expand to more APC models only after your “single device stable” path is rock solid.

Suggested “next coding step” for v9 (minimal patch, high signal)

If you want one tight v9 target that’s very likely to work without disturbing the stable USB plumbing:

  1. Add decoding stubs for 0x06, 0x14, 0x21:

    • Keep raw log (you already do)

    • Add a per-RID “last seen” and count:

      • rid_06_seen++, rid_06_last_ms = esp_log_timestamp(); etc.

  2. Extend your NUT-ish block to include:

    • driver.rid06.count, driver.rid14.count, driver.rid21.count

    • driver.last_rid, driver.last_report_ms

  3. Add a flags bit breakdown print (even if names are “BIT0/1/2/3” for now) so you can correlate with real UPS events (pull AC, overload, etc.).

If you want, paste a short snippet of the current v8 runtime logs showing a few cycles of 0x06/0x14/0x21 and I’ll propose a very surgical v9 decode scaffold that won’t touch enumeration/claiming/transfer submit logic.

Perfect — we can make this a consistent “rhythm” so every solid step produces:

    • the version header block at top

    • the REVERT index (append-only, with IDs and dates)

    • a Next Steps section at the end of the file (planned work)

Here’s the workflow I’ll follow from now on, plus templates you can copy into your repo.

Ongoing workflow (how we’ll keep context rock-solid)

After each solid step (your signal: “X works / verified / stable”)

I will produce, in the same reply:

  • Summary doc update: a new “Delta” entry + updated “Current Baseline” + refreshed “Next Steps”.

  • main.c patch guidance: exactly what to add/change while keeping your format intact.

When you start a new conversation

You paste:

  • the latest summary doc (or upload it), and

  • current main.c
    …and we continue with full continuity.

Reviewing older summary docs (evolution history)

When you upload the older summary text files, I’ll:

  • extract a timeline of milestones/versions

  • identify what changed per version

  • merge anything missing into the “Current Baseline” so nothing gets lost

(You don’t need to upload everything every time—just the latest + any historical docs you want folded in.)

Progress Summary document structure (recommended canonical format)

Use one file name and keep it updated, e.g.
ESP32S3_APC_UPS_progress_summary.txt

Template

  • Header: project goal + environment

  • Current Baseline (latest stable version): what works right now

  • Known Issues / Risks

  • Next Steps (planned)

  • Changelog (append-only): dated entries for each solid step

If you want, I can keep your exact existing structure and just enforce consistency.

main.c format conventions (locked-in)

Top of file

Keep your block exactly like you’ve been doing:

  • /* main.c CURRENT VERSION: vX ... */

  • Version tracking bullets

  • REVERT index block (append-only)

End of file

Add a permanent section like:

C
/* =========================================================
 * NEXT STEPS (planned work)
 * - [ ] v9: ...
 * - [ ] v10: ...
 * ========================================================= */

I’ll keep this updated every iteration so the file itself always tells the plan.

What I need from you each time (minimal)

Just send either:

  • “v9 is stable, here’s the log snippet” + paste updated main.c diff/sections
    or

  • upload the updated summary doc + paste main.c

And I’ll immediately return:

  • updated summary text (ready to paste/save)

  • next main.c edits + updated “Next Steps” block

Optional: a simple “Step Definition” so we don’t over-log

To avoid noise, we’ll only stamp a summary update when one of these is true:

  • confirmed stable behavior across unplug/replug or reboot

  • new decoded field validated

  • new transport/endpoint/control transfer works reliably

  • LAN exposure endpoint works (HTTP/status/etc.)

Everything else can be “scratch notes” until it’s solid.

If you upload the previous summary documents you mentioned, I’ll build a clean evolution timeline (v1→v8) and fold any missing nuggets into the current master summary so future convos never lose context.

New Documentation Rule (Locked In)

From now on every updated progress summary document will contain THREE sections at the end:

---------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION A — KNOWN WORKING BASELINE
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Summary of what is proven working in this version.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION B — NEXT DEVELOPMENT STEPS
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Concrete tasks planned for the next firmware iteration.

---------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION C — VERIFIED WORKING main.c (REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION)
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Full copy of the currently confirmed working firmware source.

So the summary file becomes:

Project Context
Hardware
Environment
Milestones
Current Status
Known Issues

SECTION A — KNOWN WORKING BASELINE
SECTION B — NEXT DEVELOPMENT STEPS
SECTION C — VERIFIED WORKING main.c

This guarantees:

✔ anyone opening the summary can rebuild the exact known-good firmware
✔ code evolution is preserved
✔ debugging always has a rollback baseline
✔ future conversations always start with full context

Version Tracking System (going forward)

Each summary will explicitly link to the firmware version.

Example:

Firmware Baseline
main.c CURRENT VERSION: v8
ESP-IDF: v5.3.1
Hardware: ESP32-S3 OTG host
UPS tested: APC 051d:0002
Status: VERIFIED WORKING

main.c Version Rules (confirmed)

Your current format is excellent and we will keep it exactly.

Required header format

/* main.c CURRENT VERSION: vX
 *
 * Version tracking:
 * vX (date): description
 *
 * REVERT INDEX
 * REVERT[ID]: explanation
 */

Required footer section

We will now always append this block to the file:

/* =========================================================
 * NEXT STEPS
 *
 * v9
 * - extend decoding for report IDs 0x06, 0x14, 0x21
 * - improve flags interpretation
 * - reduce ESP-IDF USB debug noise
 *

You said:
add all the suggested options needed for the summary report to keep clear and concise information that is repeatable by anyone

ChatGPT said:

Excellent request. What you’re asking for is essentially firmware engineering documentation discipline, which will save enormous time later (especially when reproducing builds or debugging regressions).

After reviewing your uploaded summaries and architecture docs, I recommend a standardized summary document structure that is:

  • Reproducible (anyone can rebuild firmware)

  • Traceable (milestones and fixes preserved)

  • Debuggable (REVERT references link to code)

  • Self-contained (includes the working main.c)

Your current v8 summary already contains many of these elements (hardware, environment, working status, milestones).
The architecture docs also provide network design and NUT integration context.

Below is the complete recommended template for all future summaries.

Standard Summary Document Structure (Finalized)

Every summary document should follow this exact order.

1. Document Header

Plain text
ESP32-S3 APC UPS USB HID Node
Progress Summary

Firmware Version: vX
Date: YYYY-MM-DD
Author: <optional>

Project: ESP32 NUT UPS Node
ESP-IDF Version: 5.3.1
Board: ESP32-S3
Flash: 16MB
PSRAM: 8MB

Target UPS:
Vendor: APC
VID:PID = 051d:0002

Repository Tag (optional):
git tag: ups_usb_vX

Purpose:
Allows exact environment recreation.

2. Project Goal

Short description of the system.

Example:

Plain text
Build ESP32-S3 nodes that connect to APC UPS units via USB HID and expose
UPS telemetry to the LAN using a NUT-compatible protocol and web interface.

3. System Architecture

Short overview of the whole system.

Include:

Plain text
LAN network
Central NUT server
ESP32 UPS nodes
Home Assistant integration

Example reference:

  • Orange Pi Zero 2W aggregator

  • LAN 10.0.0.0/16

  • NUT TCP port 3493

(This comes directly from your architecture documents.)

4. Hardware Configuration

Must include physical wiring.

Example:

Plain text
Board: ESP32-S3 dev board
USB ports:
 - USB-C #1 UART / flashing
 - USB-C #2 OTG host

VBUS:
OTG powered via solder bridge from power USB port

UPS cable chain:
RJ50 (UPS) → APC RJ50→USB cable → USB-A→USB-C adapter → ESP32 OTG

5. Software Environment

Required for rebuilds.

Plain text
ESP-IDF version
idf.py version
Operating system used
Serial port
Build configuration notes

Example:

Plain text
ESP-IDF v5.3.1-dirty
idf.py monitor on COM3
Flash size configured to 16MB

6. USB Device Information

Important for debugging.

Plain text
USB Speed: FULL
Device VID:PID = 051d:0002
Device Class: HID
Interface: 0
Endpoint: 0x81
Endpoint MPS: 8
Poll interval: ~100ms
Report descriptor length: 1049 bytes

These values were confirmed during your Milestone 2/3 runs.

7. Milestones & Progress

Append-only history.

Example:

Plain text
Milestone 0
Environment setup

Milestone 1
USB enumeration

Milestone 2
Interrupt IN streaming

Milestone 3
Change-only logging

Milestone 4
Basic report decoding

Milestone 5
UPS state model

Keep this chronological.

8. Verified Working Behavior

Describe exactly what works.

Example:

Plain text
UPS detected every plug-in
HID interface claimed successfully
Interrupt IN reports stream continuously
Detach / reattach works cleanly
State model resets correctly

Your v8 summary already captures this well.

9. Decoded HID Reports

Important reference table.

Example:

Plain text
RID 0x0C
Battery percent + runtime

RID 0x13
AC present

RID 0x16
Status flags

RID 0x06
Unknown heartbeat

RID 0x14
Unknown

RID 0x21
Unknown

10. Example Telemetry

Keep a few real log samples.

Example:

Plain text
0C 62 D0 89
→ batt=98%
→ runtime=35280 s

13 01
→ AC present

16 0D 00 00 00
→ flags=0x0000000D

11. Known Issues

Example:

Plain text
Control GET_DESCRIPTOR(report) may fail with ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG.
This does not affect interrupt IN streaming.
Descriptor dump disabled by default.

Your v8 summary already records this.

12. Operating Notes

Example:

Plain text
VBUS must be powered for OTG host mode.
Logging should remain change-only to prevent serial spam.
Descriptor fetch is optional for now.

13. Next Development Steps

Always list next version goals.

Example:

Plain text
v9
Decode additional report IDs (0x06, 0x14, 0x21)

v10
HTTP status endpoint

v11
Minimal NUT TCP server

v12
Web configuration interface

14. Firmware Version History

Example:

Plain text
v8
State model + NUT-style logs

v7
Descriptor dump support

v6
Stable interrupt streaming

v5
Descriptor parsing

15. Revert Reference Map

This connects documentation to main.c REVERT tags.

Example:

Plain text
REVERT[M3-A-001]
Async client callback fix

REVERT[M3-A-002]
Device descriptor retrieval fix

16. Verified Working Firmware

This is the most important rule you added.

Every summary ends with:

Plain text
---------------------------------------------------------------------
VERIFIED WORKING FIRMWARE
main.c CURRENT VERSION: vX
---------------------------------------------------------------------

Then paste the entire working main.c.

This guarantees:

  • exact reproducibility

  • regression rollback

  • reference implementation

17. End of Document Marker

Always end with:

Plain text
---------------------------------------------------------------------
END OF DOCUMENT
---------------------------------------------------------------------

Recommended File Naming

Use this format:

esp32_apc_ups_progress_v8.txt
esp32_apc_ups_progress_v9.txt
esp32_apc_ups_progress_v10.txt

Never overwrite old ones.

Optional (Highly Recommended)

Add a build reproduction block:

Plain text
Build Steps

idf.py set-target esp32s3
idf.py build
idf.py flash
idf.py monitor

Final Result

Each summary becomes a complete engineering artifact:

architecture
hardware
environment
milestones
known issues
next steps
verified firmware

Anyone could clone the repo, flash the device, and reproduce the result.

If you want, I can also build a "Master Engineering Log" format that combines:

  • architecture evolution

  • milestone log

  • firmware versions

  • testing results

into something that reads like a professional firmware development journal. It makes long projects much easier to maintain.


You said:
lets build the Master Engineering Log for record keeping of progress and confirmed successful code once done. create Me a new download document we use as the source of truth to continue to the next milestone

ChatGPT said:

Your Master Engineering Log template is ready.

Download it here:

What this document does

This file becomes the single source of truth for the project. It contains:

  • Project architecture

  • Hardware baseline

  • Software environment

  • USB device reference data

  • Milestone tracking

  • Verified behavior

  • Known issues

  • Next milestone targets

  • A permanent section for the confirmed working main.c

The key rule built into the document:

Every time a milestone is confirmed successful, the entire working main.c must be pasted into the log so the project can always be rebuilt exactly.

How we will use it going forward

Workflow for every milestone:

  1. Achieve stable firmware.

  2. Update version header in main.c.

  3. Append milestone results to the Master Engineering Log.

  4. Paste the full working main.c at the end of the document.

  5. Save a new copy of the log (or version section).

Example progression:

Master Engineering Log
 ├─ v8 (USB decode + state model)
 ├─ v9 (extended HID decoding)
 ├─ v10 (HTTP status API)
 ├─ v11 (NUT TCP server)
 └─ v12 (web configuration)

Why this is powerful

This turns the project into a reproducible engineering record:

Anyone can:

  • read the log

  • build the firmware

  • reproduce the hardware setup

  • restore the working code

No context loss between conversations or months later.

Next thing I recommend (very helpful)

We should also add a Firmware Evolution Table at the top that looks like this:

Version | Date | Milestone | Status
v8 | 2026-03-05 | Decode + state model | Stable
v9 | TBD | Extended HID decoding | In progress
v10 | TBD | HTTP API | Planned

It makes the project history instantly readable.

If you'd like, I can also build a second companion document that becomes extremely useful for embedded work:

“ESP32 UPS Firmware Debug Log”

This records:

  • test runs

  • USB traces

  • battery tests

  • AC pull events

  • runtime observations

It becomes invaluable when debugging power events later.

ChatGPT said:

Your updated Master Engineering Log (v2) is ready.

Download it here:

What was added

The new version now includes the two things you requested:

1️⃣ Firmware Evolution Table

At the top of the document you now have a table like:

VersionDateMilestoneKey AchievementsStatus
v3USB enumerationUPS detectedStable
v4HID streamingRaw reports workingStable
v8State modelNUT-style loggingCURRENT BASELINE
v9Extended decodePlannedPlanned

This gives instant visibility into project progress.

2️⃣ New Conversation Injection Workflow

A dedicated section explaining exactly how the document should be used when restarting development with a new assistant session.

It defines:

  1. Provide context

    • Upload Master Log

    • Paste main.c

    • Provide monitor logs

  2. Declare baseline

    • main.c CURRENT VERSION: vX

    • Milestone

  3. Define next milestone goal

  4. Preserve stable firmware

  5. Make incremental changes only

  6. Validate stability

  7. Update engineering log

This ensures the document remains the single source of truth for the project.

Why this structure works well

This format gives you four critical properties:

1️⃣ Reproducibility
Anyone can rebuild the firmware.

2️⃣ Traceability
Every milestone and code version is preserved.

3️⃣ Stability
Working firmware is never lost.

4️⃣ Continuity
Future conversations always know exactly where the project is.

One improvement I recommend next (very powerful)

We should add a “USB Behavior Reference Section” that records things like:

Attach sequence
Expected logs
Interrupt report patterns
Battery test results
AC failure transitions

This becomes extremely useful when debugging UPS behavior later.

If you'd like, I can also build the next document companion that professional firmware teams often keep:

UPS Firmware Test & Validation Log

It records:

  • power-fail tests

  • runtime observations

  • HID report transitions

  • UPS battery behavior

  • regression tests

It becomes invaluable once the system starts controlling shutdown automation.

ChatGPT said:

Done — I added a USB Behavior Reference section plus a Recommended Test & Validation Plan (with an append-only Validation Log and a Known Issues table that others can extend).

Download the updated document here:

What’s new in v3

  • Known Issues table (issue IDs, status, workaround pattern)

  • USB Behavior Reference:

    • expected attach sequence

You said:
if we are ready lets continue to the next step of development

ChatGPT said:

Yes — with v8 confirmed stable, the next step is Milestone 6 / firmware v9: extend decoding + tighten validation, without touching the working USB plumbing.

Your Master Engineering Log v3 already defines this as the next target (decode 0x06/0x14/0x21, improve flags interpretation, reduce noise, keep stability).

Milestone 6 (v9) — What we’ll implement now

v9 goals (safe, incremental)

  1. Keep the USB host + claim + IN reader path unchanged (don’t regress).

  2. Extend decode pipeline to track:

    • 0x06 (likely “heartbeat/status tuple”) → structured fields + counters

    • 0x14 (unknown) → counters + last payload

    • 0x21 (unknown) → counters + last payload

  3. Add timestamps + freshness so your NUT-ish status can show if data is stale.

  4. Add flags bit breakdown print-on-change (still unnamed bits, but useful for mapping).

This keeps your existing “change-only” logging behavior.

Exact code changes for v9 (drop-in edits)

1) Update the version header (top of main.c)

Add a new v9 line (keep your format):

C
/* main.c CURRENT VERSION: v9
 * Version tracking:
 *   v8 (2026-03-05): Milestone 5 start: maintain decoded UPS state + NUT-style status logs.
 *   v9 (2026-03-05): Milestone 6 start: track remaining common RIDs (0x06/0x14/0x21), add counters + freshness, add flags bit breakdown.
 */

And append REVERT entry IDs only if we discover a necessary API quirk during v9.

2) Add per-RID counters + timestamps (new globals)

Add near your “Interrupt IN tracking” section:

C
#include "esp_timer.h"   // for esp_timer_get_time()

static inline uint32_t now_ms(void) { return (uint32_t)(esp_timer_get_time() / 1000ULL); }

// RID tracking
static uint32_t s_rid06_count = 0;
static uint32_t s_rid14_count = 0;
static uint32_t s_rid21_count = 0;
static uint8_t  s_last_rid = 0;
static uint32_t s_last_report_ms = 0;

static uint32_t s_rid06_last_ms = 0;
static uint32_t s_rid14_last_ms = 0;
static uint32_t s_rid21_last_ms = 0;

// Optional: store last payload for unknown RIDs (small)
static uint8_t s_rid06_last[8]; static int s_rid06_last_len = 0;
static uint8_t s_rid14_last[8]; static int s_rid14_last_len = 0;
static uint8_t s_rid21_last[8]; static int s_rid21_last_len = 0;

3) Add “freshness” to your ups_state_t (minimal)

Extend ups_state_t:

C
typedef struct {
    bool     have_batt;
    bool     have_runtime;
    bool     have_ac;
    bool     have_flags;

    uint8_t  battery_charge_pct;
    uint32_t battery_runtime_s;
    uint8_t  ac_present;
    uint32_t flags;

    bool     on_battery;

    // v9: timestamps for freshness / stale detection
    uint32_t batt_ms;
    uint32_t runtime_ms;
    uint32_t ac_ms;
    uint32_t flags_ms;
} ups_state_t;

And on each decode, set the corresponding *_ms = now_ms().

Example inside 0x0C branch:

C
uint32_t tms = now_ms();
s_state.battery_charge_pct = batt;
s_state.battery_runtime_s  = (uint32_t)runtime_s;
s_state.have_batt = true;
s_state.have_runtime = true;
s_state.batt_ms = tms;
s_state.runtime_ms = tms;

4) Flags bit breakdown (print only when flags change)

Add a helper:

C
static void log_flags_bits(uint32_t flags)
{
    // Print bits 0..15 for now (expand later)
    char buf[128];
    int pos = 0;
    for (int b = 0; b < 16 && pos < (int)sizeof(buf) - 8; b++) {
        int set = (flags >> b) & 1U;
        pos += snprintf(&buf[pos], sizeof(buf) - pos, "%d:%d ", b, set);
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "flags bits[0..15]: %s", buf);
}

Then in your 0x16 decode branch, after logging flags:

C
log_flags_bits(flags);

(Still unnamed, but this is exactly what lets us map the meaning during AC pull tests.)

5) Track 0x06 / 0x14 / 0x21 (inside decode_and_update_state)

Add branches without assuming meaning yet:

C
else if (rid == 0x06) {
    s_rid06_count++;
    s_rid06_last_ms = now_ms();
    s_rid06_last_len = (len > 8) ? 8 : len;
    memcpy(s_rid06_last, d, (size_t)s_rid06_last_len);

    // If len>=4, log field-like view (no assumptions)
    if (len >= 4) {
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x06: b1=%u b2=%u b3=%u", (unsigned)d[1], (unsigned)d[2], (unsigned)d[3]);
    } else {
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x06: len=%d", len);
    }
}
else if (rid == 0x14) {
    s_rid14_count++;
    s_rid14_last_ms = now_ms();
    s_rid14_last_len = (len > 8) ? 8 : len;
    memcpy(s_rid14_last, d, (size_t)s_rid14_last_len);
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x14: tracked (unknown payload)");
}
else if (rid == 0x21) {
    s_rid21_count++;
    s_rid21_last_ms = now_ms();
    s_rid21_last_len = (len > 8) ? 8 : len;
    memcpy(s_rid21_last, d, (size_t)s_rid21_last_len);
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x21: tracked (unknown payload)");
}

6) Include new counters in your NUT-ish status block (only when state changes)

Extend log_nut_style_if_changed() to print:

C
ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.last_rid: 0x%02X", (unsigned)s_last_rid);
ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.last_report_ms: %" PRIu32, s_last_report_ms);
ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.rid06.count: %" PRIu32 " last_ms=%" PRIu32, s_rid06_count, s_rid06_last_ms);
ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.rid14.count: %" PRIu32 " last_ms=%" PRIu32, s_rid14_count, s_rid14_last_ms);
ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.rid21.count: %" PRIu32 " last_ms=%" PRIu32, s_rid21_count, s_rid21_last_ms);

But you need s_last_rid + s_last_report_ms updated whenever any changed report arrives. Do that in intr_in_cb right after you decide “changed”:

C
s_last_rid = d[0];
s_last_report_ms = now_ms();

7) Optional: reduce USB internal debug noise (v9 nice-to-have)

Right after usb_host_install OK:

C
esp_log_level_set("USBH", ESP_LOG_WARN);
esp_log_level_set("HUB",  ESP_LOG_WARN);

(Keep TAG logs as-is.)

v9 validation checklist (minimum to accept as new baseline)

Run the MUST tests from the log:

  • T0 Build/Flash/Boot

  • T1 Attach/Enumerate

  • T2 Steady-state streaming

  • T3 Decode sanity (0x0C/0x13/0x16 still correct)

  • T4 Detach/Reattach

Then do the SHOULD test:

  • T5 AC Pull / Restore (record flags transitions + bit breakdown)

Once that passes, we’ll:

  • mark v9 as Stable in the evolution table

  • add a validation entry

  • paste the full verified v9 main.c at the end of the Master Log

Your move (to proceed immediately)

Take the above v9 edits and apply them to your main.c. Then paste the new v9 serial log output (attach + a few “HID IN changed” cycles + detach/reattach if possible).

If you paste logs, I’ll help you interpret what 0x06/0x14/0x21 are doing and we’ll start naming bits in flags based on AC pull behavior.


You said:
moving forward lets do full main.c copy/paste drop-ins or can be compliled into a text file so I can open and copy paste after opening to keep the streaming log of the conversation from endless scrolling

ChatGPT said:

Absolutely — from here on out I’ll deliver full drop-in main.c copies (no “hunt the diff” scrolling), and I’ll also generate a downloadable text file each time so you can open → copy/paste cleanly.

v9 full drop-in main.c (copy/paste replace)

Also available as a downloadable file below.

C
/* main.c CURRENT VERSION: v9
 * Version tracking:
 *   v8 (2026-03-05): Milestone 5 start: maintain decoded UPS state + NUT-style status logs.
 *                   Disable full HID report descriptor dump by default (V7 control submit ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG).
 *                   Keep usb_host_lib_handle_events() pump + async client callback + change-only IN logging.
 *   v9 (2026-03-05): Milestone 6 start: track remaining common RIDs (0x06/0x14/0x21),
 *                   add RID counters + last-seen timestamps, add flags bit breakdown,
 *                   and add state freshness timestamps (kept out of change-detection to avoid spam).
 *
 * Notes:
 * - This is a full drop-in file (copy/paste replace main.c).
 * - USB plumbing path intentionally kept stable from v8.
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <inttypes.h>
#include <stdbool.h>

#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"
#include "freertos/semphr.h"

#include "esp_log.h"
#include "esp_err.h"
#include "esp_timer.h"   // v9: esp_timer_get_time()

#include "usb/usb_host.h"
#include "usb/usb_types_ch9.h"

// ===================== REVERT INDEX =====================
// REVERT[M3-A-001]: (2026-03-05) Use async client callback + usb_host_client_handle_events(client, timeout) (2 args).
// REVERT[M3-A-002]: (2026-03-05) Do NOT use usb_device_info_t.idVendor/idProduct (fields differ). Use usb_host_get_device_descriptor() (ptr-to-ptr).
// REVERT[M3-A-003]: (2026-03-05) IDF v5.3.1 transfer type is usb_transfer_t (NOT usb_host_transfer_t).
// REVERT[M3-A-004]: (2026-03-05) Endpoint direction test uses (ep & 0x80) instead of missing macros.
// REVERT[M3-A-005]: (2026-03-05) XFERTYPE mask macro is USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFERTYPE_MASK (not *_M).
// REVERT[M3-A-006]: (2026-03-05) NEW_DEV address saved from callback, not hardcoded.
// REVERT[M3-A-007]: (2026-03-05) No usb_host_transfer_cancel / usb_host_release_config_descriptor in v5.3.1.
// REVERT[M3-IN-001]: (2026-03-05) Change-only logging for interrupt IN reports.
// REVERT[M4-D-001]: (2026-03-05) Decode report IDs 0x0C batt/runtime, 0x13 AC, 0x16 flags (little-endian).
// =========================================================

static const char *TAG = "UPS_USB_M3";

#define UPS_VID 0x051d
#define UPS_PID 0x0002

// HID descriptor types
#define USB_DESC_TYPE_HID        0x21
#define USB_DESC_TYPE_HID_REPORT 0x22

// v8+: disable by default because V7 hit ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG on submit (likely size/path constraint)
#define ENABLE_REPORT_DESCRIPTOR_DUMP 0

static inline uint16_t rd_le16(const uint8_t *p) { return (uint16_t)(p[0] | ((uint16_t)p[1] << 8)); }
static inline uint32_t rd_le32(const uint8_t *p) { return (uint32_t)(p[0] | ((uint32_t)p[1] << 8) | ((uint32_t)p[2] << 16) | ((uint32_t)p[3] << 24)); }

static inline uint32_t now_ms(void)
{
    return (uint32_t)(esp_timer_get_time() / 1000ULL);
}

// USB host handles
static usb_host_client_handle_t s_client = NULL;
static usb_device_handle_t      s_dev    = NULL;

// Device address from NEW_DEV
static volatile uint8_t s_new_dev_addr = 0;

// HID interface/endpoint
static int      s_hid_intf_num = -1;
static uint8_t  s_ep_in_addr   = 0;
static uint16_t s_ep_in_mps    = 0;
static uint16_t s_hid_report_desc_len = 0;

// Interrupt IN tracking (change-only)
static uint8_t  s_last_in[64];
static int      s_last_in_len = -1;

// v9: report tracking
static uint8_t  s_last_rid = 0;
static uint32_t s_last_report_ms = 0;

static uint32_t s_rid06_count = 0, s_rid14_count = 0, s_rid21_count = 0;
static uint32_t s_rid06_last_ms = 0, s_rid14_last_ms = 0, s_rid21_last_ms = 0;

static uint8_t  s_rid06_last[8]; static int s_rid06_last_len = 0;
static uint8_t  s_rid14_last[8]; static int s_rid14_last_len = 0;
static uint8_t  s_rid21_last[8]; static int s_rid21_last_len = 0;

// Event flags
static volatile bool s_dev_connected = false;
static volatile bool s_dev_gone      = false;

/* =========================================================
 * Milestone 5/6: UPS state model (minimal)
 * ========================================================= */
typedef struct {
    bool     have_batt;
    bool     have_runtime;
    bool     have_ac;
    bool     have_flags;

    uint8_t  battery_charge_pct;   // from 0x0C byte1
    uint32_t battery_runtime_s;     // from 0x0C bytes2..3 (u16) but store in u32
    uint8_t  ac_present;           // from 0x13 byte1 (0/1)
    uint32_t flags;                // from 0x16 bytes1..4 (u32)

    // Derived (best-effort)
    bool     on_battery;           // derived from AC_present (if known)

    // v9: freshness timestamps (ms since boot); excluded from print-equality checks
    uint32_t batt_ms;
    uint32_t runtime_ms;
    uint32_t ac_ms;
    uint32_t flags_ms;
} ups_state_t;

static ups_state_t s_state = {0};
static ups_state_t s_state_last_printed = {0};

static bool state_equals_for_print(const ups_state_t *a, const ups_state_t *b)
{
    // NOTE: timestamps intentionally ignored to avoid status spam when values refresh unchanged.
    return (a->have_batt == b->have_batt) &&
           (a->have_runtime == b->have_runtime) &&
           (a->have_ac == b->have_ac) &&
           (a->have_flags == b->have_flags) &&
           (a->battery_charge_pct == b->battery_charge_pct) &&
           (a->battery_runtime_s == b->battery_runtime_s) &&
           (a->ac_present == b->ac_present) &&
           (a->flags == b->flags) &&
           (a->on_battery == b->on_battery);
}

static void update_derived_state(ups_state_t *st)
{
    if (st->have_ac) {
        st->on_battery = (st->ac_present == 0);
    }
}

static void log_flags_bits(uint32_t flags)
{
    // v9: simple bit view (0..15). Expand later once meanings are known.
    char buf[128];
    int pos = 0;
    for (int b = 0; b < 16 && pos < (int)sizeof(buf) - 8; b++) {
        int set = (int)((flags >> b) & 1U);
        pos += snprintf(&buf[pos], sizeof(buf) - pos, "%d:%d ", b, set);
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "flags bits[0..15]: %s", buf);
}

static void log_nut_style_if_changed(void)
{
    if (!state_equals_for_print(&s_state, &s_state_last_printed)) {
        s_state_last_printed = s_state;

        const uint32_t tms = now_ms();

        // Best-effort NUT-ish status: OL vs OB
        const char *ups_status = "UNKNOWN";
        if (s_state.have_ac) {
            ups_status = s_state.on_battery ? "OB" : "OL";
        }

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "----- NUT-ish status (Milestone 5/6) -----");

        if (s_state.have_batt) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.charge: %u", (unsigned)s_state.battery_charge_pct);
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.charge.age_ms: %" PRIu32, (uint32_t)(tms - s_state.batt_ms));
        } else {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.charge: (unknown)");
        }

        if (s_state.have_runtime) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.runtime: %" PRIu32, s_state.battery_runtime_s);
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.runtime.age_ms: %" PRIu32, (uint32_t)(tms - s_state.runtime_ms));
        } else {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "battery.runtime: (unknown)");
        }

        if (s_state.have_ac) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "input.utility.present: %u", (unsigned)s_state.ac_present);
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "input.utility.present.age_ms: %" PRIu32, (uint32_t)(tms - s_state.ac_ms));
        } else {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "input.utility.present: (unknown)");
        }

        if (s_state.have_flags) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.flags: 0x%08" PRIX32, s_state.flags);
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.flags.age_ms: %" PRIu32, (uint32_t)(tms - s_state.flags_ms));
        } else {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.flags: (unknown)");
        }

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ups.status: %s", ups_status);

        // v9 driver tracking
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.last_rid: 0x%02X", (unsigned)s_last_rid);
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.last_report_ms: %" PRIu32, s_last_report_ms);
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.rid06.count: %" PRIu32 " last_ms=%" PRIu32, s_rid06_count, s_rid06_last_ms);
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.rid14.count: %" PRIu32 " last_ms=%" PRIu32, s_rid14_count, s_rid14_last_ms);
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "driver.rid21.count: %" PRIu32 " last_ms=%" PRIu32, s_rid21_count, s_rid21_last_ms);

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "----------------------------------------");
    }
}

// Control transfer sync
typedef struct {
    SemaphoreHandle_t done;
    esp_err_t status;
    int actual_num_bytes;
} ctrl_wait_t;

// REVERT[M3-A-003]: transfer type is usb_transfer_t in IDF v5.3.1.
static void ctrl_xfer_cb(usb_transfer_t *t)
{
    ctrl_wait_t *w = (ctrl_wait_t *)t->context;
    if (w) {
        w->status = t->status;
        w->actual_num_bytes = (int)t->actual_num_bytes;
        xSemaphoreGive(w->done);
    }
}

static void intr_in_cb(usb_transfer_t *t);

// ---------- USB Host client event callback ----------
static void client_event_cb(const usb_host_client_event_msg_t *event_msg, void *arg)
{
    (void)arg;
    if (!event_msg) return;

    switch (event_msg->event) {
    case USB_HOST_CLIENT_EVENT_NEW_DEV:
        s_new_dev_addr = event_msg->new_dev.address; // REVERT[M3-A-006]
        s_dev_connected = true;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "NEW_DEV received: addr=%u", (unsigned)s_new_dev_addr);
        break;

    case USB_HOST_CLIENT_EVENT_DEV_GONE:
        s_dev_gone = true;
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "DEV_GONE received");
        break;

    default:
        break;
    }
}

// ---------- Descriptor parsing ----------
static bool parse_hid_intf_and_ep(const uint8_t *cfg, int cfg_len,
                                 int *out_intf, uint8_t *out_ep_in, uint16_t *out_mps,
                                 uint16_t *out_hid_rpt_len)
{
    int cur_intf = -1;
    bool in_hid_intf = false;
    uint16_t hid_rpt_len = 0;
    uint8_t ep_in = 0;
    uint16_t ep_mps = 0;

    int i = 0;
    while (i + 2 <= cfg_len) {
        uint8_t bLength = cfg[i];
        uint8_t bDescriptorType = cfg[i + 1];
        if (bLength < 2) break;
        if (i + bLength > cfg_len) break;

        if (bDescriptorType == USB_B_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_INTERFACE && bLength >= sizeof(usb_intf_desc_t)) {
            const usb_intf_desc_t *intf = (const usb_intf_desc_t *)&cfg[i];
            cur_intf = intf->bInterfaceNumber;

            in_hid_intf = (intf->bInterfaceClass == USB_CLASS_HID);
            if (in_hid_intf) {
                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID interface intf=%u alt=%u subclass=0x%02x proto=0x%02x",
                         (unsigned)intf->bInterfaceNumber, (unsigned)intf->bAlternateSetting,
                         (unsigned)intf->bInterfaceSubClass, (unsigned)intf->bInterfaceProtocol);
            }
        } else if (bDescriptorType == USB_DESC_TYPE_HID && in_hid_intf && bLength >= 9) {
            // HID descriptor: [6]=descType [7..8]=descLen (first class descriptor)
            uint8_t descType = cfg[i + 6];
            uint16_t descLen = rd_le16(&cfg[i + 7]);
            if (descType == USB_DESC_TYPE_HID_REPORT) {
                hid_rpt_len = descLen;
                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID Report Descriptor length = %u bytes (from HID descriptor 0x21)", (unsigned)hid_rpt_len);
            }
        } else if (bDescriptorType == USB_B_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_ENDPOINT && in_hid_intf && bLength >= sizeof(usb_ep_desc_t)) {
            const usb_ep_desc_t *ep = (const usb_ep_desc_t *)&cfg[i];

            // REVERT[M3-A-005]: use USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFERTYPE_MASK
            const uint8_t xfer_type = (ep->bmAttributes & USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFERTYPE_MASK);

            // REVERT[M3-A-004]: IN direction test uses bit7 (0x80)
            const bool is_in = ((ep->bEndpointAddress & 0x80) != 0);

            if ((xfer_type == USB_BM_ATTRIBUTES_XFER_INT) && is_in) {
                ep_in = ep->bEndpointAddress;
                ep_mps = ep->wMaxPacketSize;
                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Interrupt IN EP=0x%02x MPS=%u", (unsigned)ep_in, (unsigned)ep_mps);
            }
        }

        i += bLength;
    }

    if (cur_intf >= 0 && ep_in != 0 && ep_mps != 0 && hid_rpt_len != 0) {
        *out_intf = cur_intf;
        *out_ep_in = ep_in;
        *out_mps = ep_mps;
        *out_hid_rpt_len = hid_rpt_len;
        return true;
    }
    return false;
}

// ---------- Control GET_DESCRIPTOR (Report) ----------
static esp_err_t usb_control_get_descriptor(usb_device_handle_t dev,
                                           uint8_t bmRequestType, uint8_t bRequest,
                                           uint16_t wValue, uint16_t wIndex,
                                           uint8_t *out, int out_len,
                                           int *out_actual)
{
    if (!dev || !out || out_len <= 0) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;

    usb_transfer_t *t = NULL; // REVERT[M3-A-003]
    const int xfer_len = (int)sizeof(usb_setup_packet_t) + out_len;

    esp_err_t err = usb_host_transfer_alloc(xfer_len, 0, &t);
    if (err != ESP_OK || !t) return err;

    ctrl_wait_t w = {
        .done = xSemaphoreCreateBinary(),
        .status = ESP_FAIL,
        .actual_num_bytes = 0,
    };
    if (!w.done) {
        usb_host_transfer_free(t);
        return ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
    }

    usb_setup_packet_t *setup = (usb_setup_packet_t *)t->data_buffer;
    setup->bmRequestType = bmRequestType;
    setup->bRequest      = bRequest;
    setup->wValue        = wValue;
    setup->wIndex        = wIndex;
    setup->wLength       = (uint16_t)out_len;

    t->device_handle = dev;
    t->bEndpointAddress = 0; // EP0
    t->callback = ctrl_xfer_cb;
    t->context = &w;
    t->num_bytes = xfer_len;

    err = usb_host_transfer_submit(t);
    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        vSemaphoreDelete(w.done);
        usb_host_transfer_free(t);
        return err;
    }

    xSemaphoreTake(w.done, portMAX_DELAY);

    err = w.status;
    if (err == ESP_OK) {
        int got = w.actual_num_bytes - (int)sizeof(usb_setup_packet_t);
        if (got < 0) got = 0;
        if (got > out_len) got = out_len;
        memcpy(out, (uint8_t *)t->data_buffer + sizeof(usb_setup_packet_t), got);
        if (out_actual) *out_actual = got;
    }

    vSemaphoreDelete(w.done);
    usb_host_transfer_free(t);
    return err;
}

static void hid_get_report_descriptor_and_dump_once(void)
{
#if ENABLE_REPORT_DESCRIPTOR_DUMP
    if (!s_dev || s_hid_intf_num < 0 || s_hid_report_desc_len == 0) return;

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Descriptor dump enabled: attempting GET_DESCRIPTOR(report).");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Requesting HID Report Descriptor via control transfer: intf=%d len=%u",
             s_hid_intf_num, (unsigned)s_hid_report_desc_len);

    uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)heap_caps_malloc(s_hid_report_desc_len, MALLOC_CAP_DEFAULT);
    if (!buf) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "malloc failed for report descriptor (%u)", (unsigned)s_hid_report_desc_len);
        return;
    }

    int actual = 0;

    esp_err_t err = usb_control_get_descriptor(
        s_dev,
        0x81, 0x06,
        (uint16_t)((USB_DESC_TYPE_HID_REPORT << 8) | 0x00),
        (uint16_t)s_hid_intf_num,
        buf, (int)s_hid_report_desc_len,
        &actual
    );

    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "GET_DESCRIPTOR(report) failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
        heap_caps_free(buf);
        return;
    }

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID Report Descriptor received: %d bytes", actual);
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "\n===== HID REPORT DESCRIPTOR BEGIN =====");
    for (int i = 0; i < actual; i += 16) {
        char line[16 * 3 + 1];
        int pos = 0;
        int n = (actual - i) > 16 ? 16 : (actual - i);
        for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
            pos += snprintf(&line[pos], sizeof(line) - pos, "%02X%s", buf[i + j], (j == n - 1) ? "" : " ");
        }
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "%s", line);
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "===== HID REPORT DESCRIPTOR END =====\n");
    heap_caps_free(buf);
#else
    // default: skip to avoid control submit errors; IN reports are sufficient for Milestone 5/6.
    (void)0;
#endif
}

// ---------- Interrupt IN Reader ----------
static void decode_and_update_state(const uint8_t *d, int len)
{
    if (!d || len <= 0) return;

    uint8_t rid = d[0];
    const uint32_t tms = now_ms();

    if (rid == 0x0C && len >= 4) {
        uint8_t batt = d[1];
        uint16_t runtime_s = rd_le16(&d[2]);

        s_state.battery_charge_pct = batt;
        s_state.battery_runtime_s = (uint32_t)runtime_s;
        s_state.have_batt = true;
        s_state.have_runtime = true;
        s_state.batt_ms = tms;
        s_state.runtime_ms = tms;

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "DECODE 0x0C: batt=%u%% runtime=%u s", (unsigned)batt, (unsigned)runtime_s);

    } else if (rid == 0x13 && len >= 2) {
        uint8_t ac = d[1];
        s_state.ac_present = ac;
        s_state.have_ac = true;
        s_state.ac_ms = tms;

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "DECODE 0x13: AC_present=%u", (unsigned)ac);

    } else if (rid == 0x16 && len >= 5) {
        uint32_t flags = rd_le32(&d[1]);
        s_state.flags = flags;
        s_state.have_flags = true;
        s_state.flags_ms = tms;

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "DECODE 0x16: flags=0x%08" PRIX32, flags);
        log_flags_bits(flags);

    } else if (rid == 0x06) {
        s_rid06_count++;
        s_rid06_last_ms = tms;
        s_rid06_last_len = (len > 8) ? 8 : len;
        memcpy(s_rid06_last, d, (size_t)s_rid06_last_len);

        if (len >= 4) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x06: b1=%u b2=%u b3=%u (len=%d)",
                     (unsigned)d[1], (unsigned)d[2], (unsigned)d[3], len);
        } else {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x06: len=%d", len);
        }

    } else if (rid == 0x14) {
        s_rid14_count++;
        s_rid14_last_ms = tms;
        s_rid14_last_len = (len > 8) ? 8 : len;
        memcpy(s_rid14_last, d, (size_t)s_rid14_last_len);

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x14: tracked (unknown payload) len=%d", len);

    } else if (rid == 0x21) {
        s_rid21_count++;
        s_rid21_last_ms = tms;
        s_rid21_last_len = (len > 8) ? 8 : len;
        memcpy(s_rid21_last, d, (size_t)s_rid21_last_len);

        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "RID 0x21: tracked (unknown payload) len=%d", len);
    }

    update_derived_state(&s_state);
    log_nut_style_if_changed();
}

static void intr_in_cb(usb_transfer_t *t)
{
    if (!t) return;

    if (t->status == ESP_OK && t->actual_num_bytes > 0) {
        const uint8_t *d = (const uint8_t *)t->data_buffer;
        int len = (int)t->actual_num_bytes;

        bool changed = (len != s_last_in_len) ||
                       (memcmp(d, s_last_in, (size_t)((len > (int)sizeof(s_last_in)) ? sizeof(s_last_in) : len)) != 0);

        if (changed) {
            // REVERT[M3-IN-001]: change-only logging
            s_last_in_len = len;
            int copy_len = len;
            if (copy_len > (int)sizeof(s_last_in)) copy_len = (int)sizeof(s_last_in);
            memcpy(s_last_in, d, (size_t)copy_len);

            // v9: update last report tracking
            s_last_rid = d[0];
            s_last_report_ms = now_ms();

            char line[64 * 3 + 1];
            int pos = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < len && i < 64 && pos < (int)sizeof(line) - 4; i++) {
                pos += snprintf(&line[pos], sizeof(line) - pos, "%02X%s", d[i], (i == len - 1) ? "" : " ");
            }
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "HID IN changed (%d): %s", len, line);

            decode_and_update_state(d, len);
        }
    }

    if (!s_dev_gone) {
        (void)usb_host_transfer_submit(t);
    } else {
        usb_host_transfer_free(t);
    }
}

static esp_err_t start_interrupt_in_reader(void)
{
    if (!s_dev || s_ep_in_addr == 0 || s_ep_in_mps == 0) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;

    usb_transfer_t *t = NULL;
    esp_err_t err = usb_host_transfer_alloc(s_ep_in_mps, 0, &t);
    if (err != ESP_OK || !t) return err;

    t->device_handle = s_dev;
    t->bEndpointAddress = s_ep_in_addr;
    t->callback = intr_in_cb;
    t->context = NULL;
    t->num_bytes = s_ep_in_mps;

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting interrupt IN reader: EP=0x%02x MPS=%u (change-only)",
             (unsigned)s_ep_in_addr, (unsigned)s_ep_in_mps);

    return usb_host_transfer_submit(t);
}

/* =========================================================
 * JOB TASK: USB host library event pump
 * Why: Needed for enumeration/host actions so NEW_DEV events fire.
 * ========================================================= */
static void usb_lib_task(void *arg)
{
    (void)arg;
    while (1) {
        uint32_t flags = 0;
        esp_err_t err = usb_host_lib_handle_events(portMAX_DELAY, &flags);
        if (err != ESP_OK) {
            ESP_LOGE(TAG, "usb_host_lib_handle_events: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
        }
    }
}

/* =========================================================
 * JOB TASK: Main USB client task (open device, claim interface, start reader)
 * ========================================================= */
static void app_main_usb(void *arg)
{
    (void)arg;

    usb_host_config_t host_cfg = {
        .skip_phy_setup = false,
        .intr_flags = ESP_INTR_FLAG_LEVEL1,
    };

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(usb_host_install(&host_cfg));
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "usb_host_install OK");

    // v9 optional: reduce internal USB debug noise (keep our TAG logs readable)
    esp_log_level_set("USBH", ESP_LOG_WARN);
    esp_log_level_set("HUB",  ESP_LOG_WARN);

    // Start the library pump (critical for enumeration)
    xTaskCreatePinnedToCore(usb_lib_task, "usb_lib", 4096, NULL, 20, NULL, 0);

    usb_host_client_config_t client_cfg = {
        .is_synchronous = false,
        .max_num_event_msg = 8,
        .async = {
            .client_event_callback = client_event_cb,
            .callback_arg = NULL,
        },
    };

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(usb_host_client_register(&client_cfg, &s_client));
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "usb_host_client_register OK");

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Ready. Plug UPS into OTG port now (VBUS must be powered).");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No polling spam: logs will appear only on USB events / changed HID reports.");

    while (1) {
        (void)usb_host_client_handle_events(s_client, portMAX_DELAY);

        if (s_dev_connected) {
            s_dev_connected = false;

            if (s_new_dev_addr == 0) {
                ESP_LOGW(TAG, "NEW_DEV flag set but addr=0? skipping");
                continue;
            }

            usb_device_handle_t dev = NULL;
            esp_err_t err = usb_host_device_open(s_client, s_new_dev_addr, &dev);
            if (err != ESP_OK) {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "usb_host_device_open(addr=%u) failed: %s",
                         (unsigned)s_new_dev_addr, esp_err_to_name(err));
                continue;
            }
            s_dev = dev;

            // REVERT[M3-A-002]: get_device_descriptor returns const ptr via ptr-to-ptr
            const usb_device_desc_t *dev_desc = NULL;
            err = usb_host_get_device_descriptor(s_dev, &dev_desc);
            if (err != ESP_OK || !dev_desc) {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "get_device_descriptor failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
                usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev);
                s_dev = NULL;
                continue;
            }

            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected VID:PID=%04x:%04x bDeviceClass=0x%02x EP0_MPS=%u",
                     (unsigned)dev_desc->idVendor, (unsigned)dev_desc->idProduct,
                     (unsigned)dev_desc->bDeviceClass, (unsigned)dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize0);

            if (dev_desc->idVendor != UPS_VID || dev_desc->idProduct != UPS_PID) {
                ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Not target UPS, closing.");
                usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev);
                s_dev = NULL;
                continue;
            }

            const usb_config_desc_t *cfg = NULL;
            err = usb_host_get_active_config_descriptor(s_dev, &cfg);
            if (err != ESP_OK || !cfg) {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "get_active_config_descriptor failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
                usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev);
                s_dev = NULL;
                continue;
            }

            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Active config wTotalLength=%u", (unsigned)cfg->wTotalLength);

            s_hid_intf_num = -1;
            s_ep_in_addr = 0;
            s_ep_in_mps = 0;
            s_hid_report_desc_len = 0;

            bool ok = parse_hid_intf_and_ep((const uint8_t *)cfg, cfg->wTotalLength,
                                           &s_hid_intf_num, &s_ep_in_addr, &s_ep_in_mps,
                                           &s_hid_report_desc_len);
            if (!ok) {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to locate HID interface/EP/report-len in config descriptor");
                usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev);
                s_dev = NULL;
                continue;
            }

            err = usb_host_interface_claim(s_client, s_dev, s_hid_intf_num, 0);
            if (err != ESP_OK) {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Interface claim failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
                usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev);
                s_dev = NULL;
                continue;
            }
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Interface claimed");

            // v8/v9 default: no descriptor dump; can enable via ENABLE_REPORT_DESCRIPTOR_DUMP
            hid_get_report_descriptor_and_dump_once();

            // Reset milestone state + v9 counters on attach
            memset(&s_state, 0, sizeof(s_state));
            memset(&s_state_last_printed, 0, sizeof(s_state_last_printed));

            s_last_rid = 0;
            s_last_report_ms = 0;

            s_rid06_count = s_rid14_count = s_rid21_count = 0;
            s_rid06_last_ms = s_rid14_last_ms = s_rid21_last_ms = 0;
            s_rid06_last_len = s_rid14_last_len = s_rid21_last_len = 0;
            memset(s_rid06_last, 0, sizeof(s_rid06_last));
            memset(s_rid14_last, 0, sizeof(s_rid14_last));
            memset(s_rid21_last, 0, sizeof(s_rid21_last));

            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 5/6: state reset on attach.");

            err = start_interrupt_in_reader();
            if (err != ESP_OK) {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "start_interrupt_in_reader failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
            }
        }

        if (s_dev_gone) {
            s_dev_gone = false;

            if (s_dev) {
                if (s_hid_intf_num >= 0) {
                    (void)usb_host_interface_release(s_client, s_dev, s_hid_intf_num);
                }
                (void)usb_host_device_close(s_client, s_dev);
                s_dev = NULL;
            }

            s_new_dev_addr = 0;
            s_hid_intf_num = -1;
            s_ep_in_addr = 0;
            s_ep_in_mps = 0;
            s_hid_report_desc_len = 0;
            s_last_in_len = -1;
            memset(s_last_in, 0, sizeof(s_last_in));

            s_last_rid = 0;
            s_last_report_ms = 0;

            s_rid06_count = s_rid14_count = s_rid21_count = 0;
            s_rid06_last_ms = s_rid14_last_ms = s_rid21_last_ms = 0;
            s_rid06_last_len = s_rid14_last_len = s_rid21_last_len = 0;
            memset(s_rid06_last, 0, sizeof(s_rid06_last));
            memset(s_rid14_last, 0, sizeof(s_rid14_last));
            memset(s_rid21_last, 0, sizeof(s_rid21_last));

            memset(&s_state, 0, sizeof(s_state));
            memset(&s_state_last_printed, 0, sizeof(s_state_last_printed));
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 5/6: state cleared on detach.");
        }
    }
}

void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "=======================================");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 3: HID report monitor (change-only)");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 4: decode (0x0C batt/runtime, 0x13 AC, 0x16 flags)");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 5: state + NUT-ish status logs");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Milestone 6: track 0x06/0x14/0x21 + counters + freshness");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "ESP-IDF %s", IDF_VER);
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "main.c CURRENT VERSION: v9");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "=======================================");

    xTaskCreatePinnedToCore(app_main_usb, "app_main_usb", 8192, NULL, 10, NULL, 0);
}

/* =========================================================
 * NEXT STEPS
 *
 * v9 (Milestone 6)
 * - [ ] Observe RID 0x06/0x14/0x21 during AC pull/restore and note any field changes
 * - [ ] Start mapping flags bits to meanings (record in Master Engineering Log)
 *
 * v10 (Milestone 7)
 * - [ ] Add HTTP /status endpoint that returns JSON (read-only)
 *
 * v11 (Milestone 8)
 * - [ ] Implement minimal NUT TCP subset on port 3493 (VER, LIST UPS, LIST VAR, GET VAR, USERNAME/PASSWORD, QUIT)
 *
 * v12 (Milestone 9)
 * - [ ] Add web config UI + NVS persistence
 * ========================================================= */

Downloadable drop-in file

Open this file, copy all, paste into main.c:

Going forward (your rule)

When you ask “next step”, I’ll respond with:

  • Full drop-in main.c in a code block, and

  • a downloadable .txt version for clean copy/paste.

When you flash/run v9, paste the attach logs + a couple “HID IN changed” lines + a quick unplug/replug if you can, and we’ll confirm v9 as stable and then update the Master Engineering Log accordingly.


You said:
lets avoid any copy/paste unless absolutely needed and proceed with a downloadable drop-in file from here on out. testing v9 main.c now

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — from here on out I’ll only provide downloadable drop-in files (and I’ll keep any pasted code to an absolute minimum, only if it’s required to explain a tiny change).

You’re currently testing v9 main.c using this drop-in:

What to validate while you test v9 (quick checklist)

To accept v9 as “stable baseline”, the minimum is:

  • Attach/Enumerate: NEW_DEV → open device → parse HID intf/EP → claim → start IN reader

  • Streaming: “HID IN changed …” continues normally (no stalls)

  • Decode still correct: 0x0C / 0x13 / 0x16 decode logs still appear and make sense

  • New v9 tracking appears: RID 0x06 / 0x14 / 0x21 “tracked” logs (when those frames occur)

  • Detach/Reattach: DEV_GONE clears state cleanly; replug repeats attach sequence

When you’re done testing

Instead of pasting huge logs, you can just paste a short slice (like the lines showing):

  • startup banner (shows version v9)

  • one attach sequence

  • 1–3 “HID IN changed” lines that include 0x0C / 0x13 / 0x16 and at least one of 0x06/0x14/0x21 if possible

  • one DEV_GONE and then a reattach (if you did it)

That’s enough for me to confirm whether it’s stable and whether the new tracking is behaving as intended.

Next deliverable after v9 is confirmed

Once v9 is confirmed stable, I will generate two downloadable files (no copy/paste):

  1. Master Engineering Log update (v9 accepted baseline, validation entry, known issues updates)

  2. main_v9_verified.c.txt (same as drop-in, stamped as verified + ready to embed into the log)

Just tell me:

  • “v9 stable” (or “v9 failed with error X”), and optionally paste the small log slice above.
